title: Staff was extremely efficient
content:
In the middle of a huge strike, LH succeeded in keeping its HND-FRA flight. Boarding was fa
on time, well organized, thanks to ANA staff. Food was not exceptional. Staff was extremely efficient, and present in a long day flight through North pole; this route do not permit Wi-Fi unfortunately, but views were spectacular, and much more interesting than internet. B747 LH seats are old style, but suitable for a day flight. Upper deck offers a unique confort due to the limited quantity of people in the same space. It is a a wonderful experience, that, by itself justifies flying LH on this route.

title: shout out to the flight attendants
content: Great overall experience (food, aircraft) but especially a big shout out to the flight attendants that were polite, smiling and making sure everyone had a great flight throughout the entirety of what it was a very long flight.

title: service and food were a little disappointing
content: The first leg was on a Boeing 747-400. We received a very cheerful welcome with drinks and nuts served. The seating upstairs was old-style Business Class as expected, with limited storage and a small seatback screen. The flatbed was comfortable and full length. The vegetarian food provided was unexciting. The toilets were apparently unattended during the night flight, with toilet paper running out. The hard product was basic but comfortable, but overall the service and food were a little disappointing. The lounge we went to at Frankfurt was pleasant with a good range of food and drink, but rather crowded early in the morning. We were bussed to the aircraft -- all priority for priority passengers went out of the window as everyone piled on the bus together. Onboard the service was efficient and a tasty vegetarian lunch was served. The seating was the mean Euro-Business style. The flights were both on time and our baggage appeared on the carousel without delays.

title: vegetarian options ran out
content: No lounge in Catania, no pre boarding, vegetarian options ran out after half of the passengers in a half empty business class. No difference to economy.

title: a lacklustre experience
content: Flew from NQZ to FRA in Business on LH646. . When I arrived at NQZ airport the board stated LH check in was open but it was not and it took over 40 minutes for the LH staff to arrive. In spite of travelling C class and having Star Alliance Gold status there was no lounge access provided. Upon boarding the crew seemed suprised to see new passengers coming. They were apparently at the end of their previous leg from FRA and were apparently anxious to end their shift in ALA, hence no amenities or welcome drinks were provided and the cabin had not been cleaned. In ALA passengers to FRA were asked to leave the plane and ferried by bus to the terminal (in the middle of the night). We had to undergo additional security screening in the transit area. In ALA the crew changed and the new crew appeared to be only mildly motivated. As the flight leaves in the very early hours of the morning there was no meaningful food service until breakfast about 2 hours before landing. Breakfast was decent but not in line with the enhanced customer experience LH had announced as of May 2026. The flight landed approx 90 minutes late in FRA due to a technical issue in NQZ. My bag was one of the last to be delivered at the baggage belt although being marked as "priority". Unfortunately a lacklustre experience.

title: crew were friendly and attentive
content: Business Class from Frankfurt to Astana on a 14 year old A330. Chose this connection because Lufthansa is the only major western carrier apart from TK to serve NQZ. Boarding in Frankfurt was fairly chaotic and consisted of a transfer with crowded bus to an apron position (no dedicated bus for C class). The flight was on time and uneventful, however the old LH business class product is a far cry from being competitive. The crew were friendly and attentive, food and drink were ok, but nothing to write home about. All in all a rather mediocre experience.

title: Staff was exceptional
content: Nice to Osaka via MUC was the quickest way for the route. An expensive ticket. Boarding in MUC was delayed and chaotic. Onboard the seats were as expected, not of great comfort but the A359 was so silent and pleasant. Staff was exceptional. Our trouble was food. We had pre-ordered meals for the MUC-KIX flight but the pre-ordered meals (lunch) were not delivered to us. And there was only one remaining option when they realized it. 2 hours before landing, there were not enough breakfast set for all Business class passengers.

title: forced me to check my bag
content: On flight LH193 from Berlin to Frankfurt I was subjected to an arbitrary decision at the check-in counter. Despite having the right to carry-on luggage, the staff forced me to check my bag. I stated that the bag contained medications, my laptop, and delicate collector's ceramics. My concerns were met with total indifference. To make matters worse, once I boarded, the cabin was half-empty. There was plenty of room in the overhead bins for my luggage.

title: Food was very average
content: The flight from Frankfurt to Hong Kong was on a Boeing 787. It was a full and tough flight. It was 11 hour and 15 minutes. Take off was 30mins late, but arrived 2 minutes after arrival time. Quite a good selection on the IFE. Food was very average. The cabin crew weren t very friendly. I didn t like them walking around with torches as they would shine it in your face waking you up. It had WiFi give was great for messaging apps. On arrival at Hong Kong airport the taxi to get to the gate was crazy, it took 30 minutes. It s a common thing apparently.

title: a rather uneventful flight
content: This Lufthansa flight from London Heathrow to Frankfurt was a rather uneventful flight. It was a few minutes over an hour to arrive. The seats and the carpet looked rather new. You were given a free bottle of water during the flight, there was buy on board available. Left on time and arrived 15mins early

title: two-hour delay awaiting my luggage
content: I endured a two-hour delay awaiting my luggage. Despite the display at the baggage carousel indicating a 'finished' status, my items had not arrived, necessitating a visit to the Lufthansa Service Desk. The staff there appeared overburdened After a protracted wait, I was informed that the luggage had been processed and was instructed to retrace my steps entirely to the reclaim area.

title: Avoid LH if there is a choice
content: The legspace in the A319 is not different from Ryanair, Service is hardly any better: we got a small bottle of water and a tiny piece of chocolate. Welcomed by two air hostesses who were struggling to interrupt their chat to welcome passengers. One passenger had the audacity to ask for some water before taking off and was told to wait. On arrival one of the crew members was on her phone in stead of saying goodbye to the passengers. On the positive side: the flight landed 15 minutes earlier in Amsterdam and they have a good app with good information. I also liked the digital luggage tag. Would I recommend Lufthansa? The leg Munich-Amsterdam was the final of a trip from Asia. Having spent three weeks there and having flown with Middle Eastern and Asian airlines the answer is simple: no. Avoid LH if there is a choice.

title: Lufthansa is reliable
content: FRA-LHR return. The first flight began stressful with two gate changes and a lot of walking through the terminal. The service onboard was minimal. You get a small bottle of water and a piece of chocolate. Food and drinks for purchase were offered, but the snack I was interested in was sold out. The flights were on time (more or less) and the crews were nice. Overall Lufthansa is reliable but the inflight experience is rather poor.

title: Lufthansa doesn’t care
content: IFE failed on a 14 hour flight. First class was booked and catered to 4 - but then 4 upgrades at gate so not enough food. Vegetarian options very very limited and quality was mediocre. Lufthansa s response to the service failure? 8300 points - equivalent to 25 off a next flight. So Lufthansa doesn t care or value its First Class catering or IFE.

title: stupid stress generating rule
content: Checking in at BER with carry on 10 kilo so 2 kilos over the limit. When I flew business nobody did even check the weight or size last time. I had to take out my necessities and meds blocking the counter. I guess my carry on went down to 9 kilo by then but it had to go on a journey. My bag been checked all the way to IAH. Been loaded on the belt go through system to be loaded to the plane BER-FRA. Had to be unloaded in FRA transferred to United plane in FRA and loaded in there to make its way to IAH. My question is why making it miserable for all involved waisting time, manpower and forcing meaningless stress. I do not understand the meaning behind al this stupid stress generating rule.

title: Won’t refund it
content: As a Senator level there s no way to speak to somebody. Everything must be submitted online and nobody replies. Ripped off, sold me an expensive (double) economy ticket that I could upgrade it with vouchers and miles. Once I d paid the money the upgrade was no longer available. Won t refund it, nobody to complain to.

title: airport mishandled our bags
content: The agent at the business class check in at Berlin airport mishandled our bags, which resulted in a great amount of stress. We checked four bags with her, and she made two mistakes, checked two bags under the same claim number and apparently did not tag one of our bags. When I did check the claim tickets she gave me - which were stickers stuck together - I realized that she only gave us 3 tags for the 4 bags. Thus began our odyssey of trying to track our fourth bag which had special items in it. We started with the agent at the business class lounge, and she attempted to help us. Long story short, every person we spoke to along our journey - other than her - was unhelpful. We had an Apple AirTag in our bag and could show them exactly where our bag was - sitting in Berlin airport. We offered to share the tracking information with them but they refused to accept it. Then we arrive in SFO and fill out a missing bag claim with their "lost and found." That person blamed other parties and systems and was unhelpful. I acknowledge that our flights were on time and the service in the business class cabin from Munich to SFO was quite attentive and good. The wifi was incredibly slow and why they charged us for it when we already were paying an exorbitant fare I cannot understand. Eventually our bag was delivered to our home..

title: sufficient legroom and seat width
content: Check-in smooth but unfortunately unable to select seats as flights were part of Air India codeshare. Luckily it resulted in an aisle and a window seat. Delhi-Munich on the A380 left almost an hour late but managed to arrive just in time. Plane was rather old, even no USB charging options, but clean and sufficient legroom and seat width. The IFE was just as dated but didn't bother me too much as this was a night flight. Service from the crew was spot on, fast distribution of the meals and timely cleanup and with several passages for additional drinks. Even received a hot towel, long time since I've seen this in economy. Got a tight 50 minutes layover in Munich, but it took only 20 minutes in total from disembarking the first flight to being on board the next flight - this including clearing security and immigration! Very efficient airport and would for sure prefer it over a 2 hours layover in Frankfurt. A319 to Brussels - standard no thrills short EU flight, arrived on time and the baggage made it so that's what matters.

title: much better than I thought it would be
content: A short flight so fairly bare-bones, but no complaints at all. I was concerned about seat comfort and pitch, but I was pleasantly surprised by the width and legroom - and I'm 6'2". I could have easily been comfortable on a longer flight. Plane was very clean, and boarding was efficient. Cabin crew served beverages. The experience was much better than I thought it would be.

title: just a free middle seat
content: Short flight and on time, all good but only the fact that short haul European business class seating is just a free middle seat is strange. A world airline could do much better in the short haul segment.

title: they ran out of sandwiches
content: Dissapointing. On both legs of our journey from Krakow to London via Munich they ran out of sandwiches after serving the first few rows. The plain was delayed almost an hour before take off from krakow so we did not have time enough to eat anything on the stopover airport in Munich. We paid for the Wi-Fi connection and expected it will last for both legs of the journey but you have to pay separately for each flight.

title: Extremely disappointing service
content: My flight from Frankfurt to Berlin was cancelled, and at the airport our luggage was not returned to us. Because we urgently needed to reach Berlin, we had to rent a car and drive ourselves. From that point on, the handling of the baggage situation was unacceptable. I filed a delayed baggage report and clearly stated several times that my suitcase contained urgent medication and that my stay in Berlin was limited. While other passengers from the same flight received their luggage or were even able to collect it themselves, mine was not delivered. Using an AirTag, I could see that my luggage was at Berlin airport, yet Lufthansa s emails continued to say it was not located. I called repeatedly, shared the AirTag location when requested, and explained the urgency again and again. Nothing happened. I later received a delivery notice for a time when I had already clearly said I would no longer be in Berlin. Lufthansa admitted a mistake had been made but said nothing could be changed because the bag was already with the courier. The courier and subcontractors then passed responsibility back and forth, with no one able to help. Despite very clear communication from the beginning including the fact that the bag contained medication Lufthansa and its partners failed to coordinate even a basic delivery or update the destination. The luggage is still not with me. Extremely disappointing service.

title: my last flight with this airline
content: Tried checking in online so we wouldn't have to stand in line for almost an hour at the BUD airport. Their system doesn't recognize the second largest city in Colorado, hence can't enter my address and check in online. Standing in line for almost an hour. Then they seat me and my child separately on both flights. Their system wouldn't let the person behind the desk to change the tickets. Even though there were two seats next to each other. Today is my last flight with this airline.

title: A disappointing flight
content: A disappointing flight, only redeemed by the efforts and attitude of the cabin staff. To start with, LH reservation system automatically assigned seats on the opposite sides of the cabin to me and my wife (it being the same booking) - and wanted 60 EUR to have this changed. Onboard, the LH A350-900 business class configuration was somewhat funny trying to give window access to all side seats; as a result, the individual cabins felt claustrophobic and surprisingly uncomfortable, both as seats and as beds. Entertainment system misbehaved in that the films or music could not be paused. WiFi was also out of order, for which LH apologized by sending a mail. Having boarded just after midnight, we skipped dinner but the vegan breakfast was so sad looking (a photo attached) that we never regretted having done so. There was no wardrobe to hang the overcoats. Only two toilets were available for the whole of business class cabin.

title: requested cash compensation
content: I flew Lufthansa First Class from the USA to Europe. My baggage was damaged during transport. I filed the damage report and followed the process as instructed. Lufthansa responded once and then went silent. It has now been twelve days of being ignored, despite follow-ups. No update. No resolution. Their idea of compensation was to suggest replacement luggage if I paid extra money myself. When I rejected this proposal and requested cash compensation, Lufthansa simply stopped replying. This is not a baggage issue. This is a customer service failure at a systemic level. Anyone considering Lufthansa for premium travel should understand once there is a problem, you are on your own.

title: configuration 2-2-2 leaves much to be desired
content: The FRA-BOS route typically uses an older plane and a very old business class seating configuration. While the cabin service was excellent, the business class configuration 2-2-2 leaves so much to be desired. Four out of 6 in each row do have aisle access (without crawling over sleeping humans as on much of BA business): a small plus. But the seats when stretched flat (with old-fashioned quirky controls that needed to be rebooted once for my pair of seats) left me with my feet (5-10 height) on a small shelf barely divided from the feet of the person sitting next to me. We could have played footsie but he was and remains a stranger - a very nice person, we had a brief conversation at the outset and parting. The seat itself was narrow, there was no seat cover in the linen pack (unlike my previous YUL-CMN AC business class flight) but only a tired "duvet" and a pillow. On a well-worn seat. At a discounted price, this might have been a deal. But at business class rates, LH should really up their game and consider putting either a newer plane or a new 2025/2026 business class seating configuration into place.

title: Mid grade at best
content: Mid grade at best. Staff could be better. Bathrooms can be cleaner. We travelled with an infant and the staff seemed least bothered to make us more comfortable.

title: now feels like a lottery
content: On 24 July (Munich Stockholm), I had a confirmed boarding pass but was moved to the waiting list 15 minutes before take-off. I was initially told it was for unavoidable security reasons, but Lufthansa later admitted it was due to overbooking where I experienced a 4 hour delay to reach my destination. On 7 October (Athens Munich), my seat was changed at the last minute with poor reasons provided, my luggage was lost, and staff repeatedly gave me the runaround, sending me from one desk to another without resolving anything. Employees were rude and unhelpful, acting as if assisting passengers was a favour rather than a duty. Flying Lufthansa now feels like a lottery confirmed seats mean nothing, overbooking is standard, staff are dishonest, and customer service is abysmal.

title: first and last time
content: The first and last time I ever fly with the Lufthansa. On July 8th, 2025, LH446 got diverted to SLC, turning the 10h flight into a 16h+ disaster. My party was coming in from a tight connection and we made it by a hair. We made it, our bags did not. No big deal, just see an agent upon arrival, or so we thought. The Lufthansa service area is vacant. Next option: call customer service. A member of my party called the hotline, and the person on the other end proceeded her into thinking it was in the lost and found, or that it was another airline's fault. Luckily, their website offered somewhat of an answer, and we all submitted baggage research claims, and received our bags 72h later at 1AM.

title: customer relations are appalling
content: Lufthansa lost my luggage on a flight within Europe. Inconvenient, but mistakes happen. The airport s lost luggage officer was very helpful and filed a report immediately. A week later an airport officer advised that the bag had turned up and I was able to collect it from the airport. It had a tag on it showing that it had been sent to the wrong airport. So why I am writing this? Well, my issue is that Lufthansa made no attempt to contact me at the time and despite months of trying, no apology or compensation. Lufthansa knew about the loss because the airport had sent them a report and there was a note on the Lufthansa website saying that the loss had been reported. But I was given zero information about whether my luggage was still in transit or lost for good. The website would not issue a tracking number because of the airport report. A few days later, assuming that the baggage would not be returned, I made a compensation claim, for which I simply received a proforma request for more information, without acknowledging that they already knew where the bag was. Months later, still with nothing from Lufthansa, my travel agent advised me to lodge a feedback form. Once again, nothing happened. When the agent followed up, Lufthansa s offered only platitudes, implying that reporting of the luggage loss and the problems with their tracking and contact system were my fault. They still have never acknowledged that they knew of the loss, let alone acknowledge that I am out of pocket. Months later, they are even asking for proof of the airport s lost luggage report, even though they were advised on the day of the flight and they eventually found the luggage and forwarded it. I have no complaints about the cabin staff or on-board meals. But their customer relations are appalling.

title: plane had flown without our luggage
content: Lufthansa operated Flight LH1868 from Munich to Rome on October 30 without loading any passenger baggage and did not inform the passengers, and no communication on arrival. The baggage monitors in Rome displayed an assigned carousel for our flight, so I waited in confusion. Only after landing did we learn that the plane had flown without our luggage and the bags would be sent to FCO on a later flight. We then spent five hours at the airport waiting for bags to be delivered from Munich. This could have been handled with a simple announcement and clear process, but passengers were left uninformed. I ve filed a formal complaint.

title: immediately arranged for a hotel
content: Unfortunately, our flight was delayed by 24 hours due to one of the pilots' illness. However, Lufthansa immediately arranged for a hotel, transportation, and meals. The aircraft didn't show its age of about 20 years. It was in extremely good mechanical condition. The food and drinks were appropriately good for a long-haul flight.

title: flight attendants were fantastic
content: Both flight attendants on this flight were fantastic. While their business class product isn't up to the same standard as Australia, the crew made up for it in particular the female. She just made everything look easy, incredibly efficient with a level of professionalism in a calm and smooth manner The pilot was very communicative and even met some passengers as we were boarding. Overall the flight was fantastic .

title: low cost version of Lufthansa
content: On my journey from Tbilisi to Paris, I had a connecting flight from Munich operated by Lufthansa City. I was genuinely surprised by the stark contrast between the crew on this leg and the one on my earlier flight from Tbilisi to Munich. While the first crew was warm and engaging, the team on the Munich Paris flight seemed completely disengaged. No smiles, no communication, just a mechanical execution of duties. It felt like they were going through the motions without any human touch. Lufthansa City looks like low cost version of Lufthansa in business class. Very sad.

title: fell short of expectations
content: I flew LH1779 from Lisbon to Munich on 14 Aug 2025, connecting to Athens. Check-in was smooth and free Wi-Fi a plus, but after that the experience fell short of expectations. The Lufthansa app showed the wrong gate, forcing us to run between gates. The flight left late, and when we asked crew for help making our tight connection, the response was dismissive ( I can t make the plane fly faster ). Onboard, even coffee required payment, which felt more budget airline than premium. In Munich, our app showed the connecting flight still available, but the gate had closed seven minutes before departure. Staff at the gate offered no help, and the Service Centre rebooked us for the next day with a very basic hotel and poor instructions for transport. Lufthansa had assured us before booking that 50 minutes was enough for a connection in Munich, but in reality with early gate closures and delays you only have ~25 minutes. Future travelers should plan for at least 90 minutes or more when connecting in Munich or Frankfurt.

title: acknowledge the high professionalism
content: Travelled from Tbilisi to Munich with Lufthansa (late afternoon flight). The check in process at Tbilisi was good. The experience at boarding gate was chaotic (this is every time issue in Tbilisi). The flight itself was excellent and I would like to acknowledge the high professionalism and friendliness of the crew. Senator status was also acknowledged by the crew. Thank you very much. Food service has been improved with three main choices and better quality. Pillow, blanket, hot towel, food, beverages - everything helped to have a good flight.

title: refused to give me a refund
content: I flew from Frankfurt to Israel with Lufthansa, and my seat was broken. There was a bump in the middle of the cushion. Lufthansa s customer service is a joke. There s no phone number, emails go unanswered for weeks, and when I finally heard back, they refused to give me a refund or any compensation. I ll

title: bags are still missing
content: Disappointing Experience On August 15, 2025, my partner and I flew from Dublin to Cairo via Frankfurt on Lufthansa. We checked two bags, but upon arrival in Cairo, neither arrived. At the airport, we filled out lost luggage forms and were promised delivery within 2 days. It s now day 5, and our bags are still missing, with no clear updates from Lufthansa. We ve had to repeatedly contact customer service ourselves, only to receive vague responses or none at all. This lack of communication and accountability from a supposed first-world airline is unacceptable. Our trip ends in 2 days, and the absence of our luggage has severely disrupted our plans, forcing us to replace essentials at our own expense. Lufthansa s failure to deliver on their promise or provide timely updates has turned our trip into a frustrating experience. I urge other travelers to be cautious when flying with Lufthansa and to share similar experiences to hold the airline accountable. I m still waiting for a resolution and expect better from a premium carrier.

title: upset by the company's attitude
content: I was flying from Barcelona to Gda sk with a connecting flight in Munich. Because the first flight was delayed, it was physically impossible to make the connection. The airline offered an alternative flight departing a few hours later. Since I ultimately arrived in Gda sk 4 hours later than scheduled, I was entitled to compensation. When I wrote to the airline about this, they agreed and asked for all the necessary bank details in order to send me this compensation. The amount was supposed to arrive within 10 days, but more than a month has already passed and the money has not arrived. I wrote to them about it via email, but they do not respond. Every one of my emails is ignored. I have written to various email addresses of theirs, no one answers, and this issue is not being resolved in any way. I am very upset by the company's attitude.

title: Paid for upgrade to business
content: Paid for upgrade to business class ($712,54) and was promised three things: An elevated dining experience; A comfortable lie-flat bed; Access to the Business Class Lounges. The catering onboard was without question below average, offered only one choice of starter (meat and cream cheese), three choices for meal, and one choice for dessert (fruit plate and cheese are not dessert items). I ordered the Shrimp and Scallop stir-fry, which turned out to be about the same quality and taste profile as a reheatable meal. The dessert I choice, bread pudding, was not a bread pudding but a cold square of an apple cake. The lie-flat bed was extremely uncomfortable narrow, not flat, and inadequately cushioned. I was refused entry to the business class lounge at Los Angeles, Chicago O Hare, & Flughafen Frankfurt-Rhein-Main for specious reasons I do not recall. I was publicly embarrassed three times when making the request for entry.The ground services at O'Hare was just chaotic and unprofessional. This agent barring passengers from this seating area, boarding passengers in three different areas, and, of course, American-style customer service (meaning no one knows how to talk to the public). I will mention here that staff were as always very kind and gracious. Just another "basic airline" that wants the revenue from premium passengers without providing the service.

title: claim our travel insurance
content: We can understand that the cancelation which is because of Typhoon. 500+ flights canceled and we needed to wait on 3-days for return flight which was understandable. However, to ask for an official letter for the flight cancelation in terms of we can claim our travel insurance. It's a ping pong play between Swiss, Lufthansa and Cathay Pacific. It's been two weeks now and they are still pointing fingers to each other. The whole thing with emails all time. Just asked for an official letter with date, time and reason of cancelation. We went through times Customer hotlines with Lufthansa, Customer hotlines with Cathy Pacific. Multiple emails with all three airlines and still nothing. If the airlines can't make it right, please don't provide such option.

title: staff at the gate showed no empathy
content: I was denied boarding on flight LH1710 from Munich to Dubrovnik on August 2nd due to overbooking. While I understand that overbooking can happen, the way it was handled was unacceptable. The ground staff at the gate showed no empathy or effort to explain the situation. Passengers who arrived later at the gate than I did were allowed to board, and I was placed on standby without any clear explanation or fair prioritization. Only after I insisted was I given a confirmation that the denial of boarding was due to overbooking. I received compensation, but not a full explanation or the full rights I was entitled to under EU Regulation 261/2004.

title: flight delayed until the next day
content: We taxied to the runway, only for the plane to abruptly slow down and return. We remained seated on the plane for over 3 hours, during which we received conflicting and increasingly concerning updates. After one hour We need to refuel since we were on the ground too long. After another hour: We are having engine problems. After yet another hour: We will switch you to a different aircraft. Finally, it was announced the flight would be delayed until the next day, and that we would be taken to a hotel for the night. We had two small children with us, and being confined on the plane for three hours with no food, no clear updates, and no solution was both exhausting and distressing. The situation worsened once we deplaned. I told the service desk representative that I could not continue walking long distances, as we had already been up and down stairs multiple times. They said they would arrange for a cart to transport us through the airport but we were left waiting for an hour. When the cart finally arrived, I was told no children were allowed, so we were forced to walk across the airport. The real reason for the delay was only revealed the next day: Customs in St. Louis was closed. Why was this not communicated from the start?

title: too late to check our bags
content: We recently had a terrible experience at Toronto Pearson Airport while traveling with our four-year-old daughter. We arrived at the airport 1.5 hours before our international flight to Sofia (via Germany). We asked the Lufthansa service desk if there was a fast lane or any priority process for families with young children as there was only 1.5 hours left before our flight departed. We were told no and directed to a long line marked Bag Drop Only. After waiting over 30 minutes in line, we reached the counter with 1 hour left before takeoff, only to be told it was "too late" to check our bags. The staff offered no assistance other than telling us we could throw away our checked luggage and run to the gate with only carry-ons. The supervisor showed zero empathy, refused to listen, and ultimately blocked us from flying. We pleaded for understanding and assistance, but were met with cold, rigid responses and no effort to help. There was no fast lane, no signage warning of cut-off times, and no ownership from Lufthansa for their poor process at the airport. We had to pay out of pocket to rebook everything. For a premium airline, Lufthansa s customer service was anything but professional.

title: negligent about their help service
content: The flight was fine and the flight crew are friendly and nice to passengers. However Lufthansa's customer service is the most awful I have encountered. I was bumped off of my outbound flight (with the wording of the Lufthansa app implying that the flights were cancelled outright) from LHR to BER with a stopover in BRU onto a flight I could not make, and Lufthansa has been incredibly incompetent and unhelpful in sorting it out, nearly always redirecting me to Brussels Airlines, who operated the flight, who then redirect me back to Lufthansa because it was a Lufthansa booking. Lufthansa's customer service don't seem to understand that the two airlines operate within the same group, nor do they at all give the impression that they care about helping you. I wrote this review to make sure that Lufthansa does not get away with the fact that they are clearly completely negligent about their help service. You're fine if you don't have any issues on your flight, but if you do, god help you, because Lufthansa certainly won't. If you find yourself in this situation, ask for Nate at the Lufthansa help center. They have been the only helpful Lufthansa employee in this matter.

title: checked bags did not make the connection
content: TripVerified The flight to Frankfurt goes smoothly, but we almost miss our connecting aircraft because we arrived at gate A1, and our next plane was at Z99. As if almost missing the connection was bad enough, the plane diverted to Salt Lake City due to a storm in Denver, turning the 10h flight into a 15h flight. After all that, and a good half hour of waiting at the customs baggage carousel, we learn that our checked bags did not make the connection with us. Our bags finally arrived at our house, over 36h late, but that means next to nothing now. I had to delete my miles & more account because of this.

title: unacceptable experience"
content: Washington Dulles to Munich on Lufthansa A380 Business class award ticket obtained from UA Mileage Plus program. The Lufthansa lounge in Washington Dulles is very disappointing, with a poor choice of rather low quality foods. This was a late flight (10:20 pm) and our goal was to have dinner in the lounge to go to sleep after takeoff. The food offering was what you can expect from a school cafeteria in a country with limited financial resources. The only positive points were the flight attendants, who were pleasant and attentitve, and the pre-departure nuts and chocolate candy, which were of good quality. The lie-flat seat was atrocious and breakfast was forgettable. The very dated side-by-side arrangement did not bother us but the seat itself was narrow and encumbered with protruding metal parts which made falling asleep quasi-impossible. Result, an exhausting 8-hour flight and a totally unacceptable experience overall for a business class transatlantic trip. The flight left and arrrived essentially on-time.

title: cabin crew was outstanding
content: My penultimate flight with Lufthansa in First Class was impressive in every respect not only because of the high level of comfort, but above all because of the exceptionally dedicated staff. Even the stay in the First Class terminal in Frankfurt was a very pleasant start. The service was unobtrusive, efficient, and personal everything ran smoothly, from check-in to the direct ride to the aircraft with the limousine service. This impression continued on board. I would particularly like to highlight the purser, who introduced herself personally, was present throughout the entire flight, and kept an overview at all times without appearing distant. She had a few personal words for each guest professional yet warm. The welcome from the captain (before takeoff, of course), who personally took the time to introduce himself, was a strong signal of the appreciation he felt for the First Class guests. This kind of closeness to the crew is something you rarely experience and it made a noticeable difference. The cabin crew was outstanding: attentive, courteous, and always one step ahead without being intrusive. It was noticeable that this was a well-coordinated team that truly enjoyed their work. The seat or rather, the little private sanctuary offered everything you could wish for on a long-haul flight: plenty of space, high-quality materials, pleasant peace and quiet, and a very comfortable bed. Even after many hours, I felt fresh and relaxed. The catering was consistently of a high standard. The caviar service was classic and very well presented, the dishes were delicious and beautifully presented. The selection of drinks especially the wines and champagne was also high-quality and well-curated. In conclusion, Lufthansa First Class impresses not only with its exceptional comfort and quality, but above all with people who take their work seriously and do it with passion. It was a journey where every detail was perfect calm, well-thought-out, and to the point. Mein vorletzter Flug mit Lufthansa in der First Class war in jeder Hinsicht beeindruckend nicht nur wegen des hohen Komforts, sondern vor allem durch das au ergew hnlich engagierte Personal. Bereits der Aufenthalt im First Class Terminal in Frankfurt war ein h chst angenehmer Einstieg. Der Service war unaufdringlich, effizient und pers nlich alles lief reibungslos, vom Check-in bis zur direkten Fahrt zum Flugzeug mit dem Limousinen-Service. An Bord setzte sich dieser Eindruck fort. Besonders hervorheben m chte ich die Purserin, die sich pers nlich vorstellte, den gesamten Flug ber pr sent war und jederzeit den berblick behielt, ohne dabei distanziert zu wirken. Sie hatte f r jeden Gast ein paar pers nliche Worte brig professionell und gleichzeitig herzlich. Auch die Begr ung durch den Kapit n (nat rlich noch vor dem Start), der sich pers nlich Zeit nahm, um sich vorzustellen, war ein starkes Signal f r die Wertsch tzung gegen ber den First-Class-G sten. Diese Art von N he zur Crew erlebt man selten und sie machte einen sp rbaren Unterschied. Das Kabinenpersonal war hervorragend: aufmerksam, zuvorkommend und immer einen Schritt voraus, ohne aufdringlich zu wirken. Es war sp rbar, dass hier ein eingespieltes Team mit echter Freude an der Arbeit unterwegs war. Der Sitz oder besser gesagt, das kleine private Refugium bot alles, was man sich auf einem Langstreckenflug w nschen kann: viel Platz, hochwertige Materialien, angenehme Ruhe und ein sehr bequemes Bett. Auch nach vielen Stunden f hlte ich mich frisch und entspannt. Das Catering war durchweg auf hohem Niveau. Der Kaviarservice war klassisch und sehr gut pr sentiert, die Speisen geschmacklich berzeugend und sch n angerichtet. Auch die Auswahl an Getr nken insbesondere die Weine und der Champagner war hochwertig und gut kuratiert. Fazit: Lufthansa First Class berzeugt nicht nur durch ausgesprochenen Komfort und Qualit t, sondern vor allem durch Menschen, die ihre Arbeit ernst nehmen und mit Leidenschaft ausf hren. Es war eine Reise, bei der jedes Detail stimmte unaufgeregt, durchdacht und auf den Punkt.

title: boarding process at Frankfurt gets worse
content: I fly this route on LH three times a year. Each time, the boarding process at Frankfurt gets worse. Chaos, confusion, not boarding by groups, it looked like they opened the gates a a football stadium and there was pushing, shoving and no consideration for small children or people with mobility issues. Why can't they get it right? It's so easy. On board, the staff is very courteous even in the face of catering issues. Several of the items offered on the menu for dinner were not boarded. A chicken piccata appeared as a slice of chicken covered in green sauce. Breakfast was a write-off. Coffee warm/tepid. I fly LH because it gets me to my destination on time. Not because of the inflight entertainment on outdated screens of the tired cabins. LH needs to improve to compete with the other European airlines flying to EZE. Great checkin staff at EZE - to be commended.

title: expensive for minimal service
content: Okay flight, as usual very expensive for minimal service, only water is included in 3hr flight. Terrible ground crew in Frankfurt, and delays create a terrible value for money

title: Excellent service, terrible seats
content: Excellent service but terrible seats. Flew DC to Frankfurt. Great food and wonderful, attentive service but the lie flat seats were very uncomfortable, hard, and not conducive to sleep. In flight entertainment desperately needs updating.

title: crew were friendly and efficient
content: Both flights were on time and were both very full. However the seats were comfortable for the fairly short flights. Were given a bottle of water and a small branded Lindt Chocolate on both flights. Cabin crew were friendly and efficient. Would fly again.

title: baggage has not arrived
content: I did not get the opportunity to agree to the new flight schedule but Lufthansa went ahead and confirmed the changes and issued boarding passes. I m perplexed to who in their right mind would think it reasonable to schedule a 24 hour transit time. At check-in I managed to negotiate a route to bring the delay down to 12 hours with transit times in two airports of 5 and 6 hours respectively. Find at destination baggage has not arrived.

title: too expensive for the value
content: Both flights on time. Lounge in HAJ was ok for small airport. On the 30 mins flight to FRA they manage to serve a small dish, carrot tartare. Looks and taste perfect. The new lounge in FRA near the Z gates is a clear improvement. Cabin in A 340 still like ten years ago. 2-2-2 configuration. Seats are comfortable. IFE technic outdated. Crew was very attentive. Food was tasty and good choice. Overall the Business Class in the A 340 is average as outdated. Nevertheless seats are comfortable. But compared to many asian or mideast airlines too expensive for the value.

title: tried to claim the money
content: The only possibility to get to Munich from Barcelona (24th of March till 27th of March) was Lufthansa. First time with them and it will be the last time. I arrived at 14:35 at Munich and bagage drop off was (according boarding pass) possible till 14:45. Unfortunately the lady at the counter said, this is only for business class. At the service counter they offered me a flight through Zurich to Barcelona. Total costs EURO 250. After my flight tried to claim the money. The only reaction was, sorry for you and this will help us to avoid those situations. No offer or refund.

title: certain Lufthansa would reimburse me"
content: I purchased business class seats from NYC to Frankfurt to Malta. Total expected flight time was 12 hours, 22 minutes. Due to a 3.5 hour delay out of JFK, I missed my connecting flight from Frankfurt. I was re-routed to Rome to catch a flight to Malta. I waited at the airport in Frankfurt 3 1/2 hours and boarded the flight to Rome. The flight to Rome was delayed so I missed my re-routed 9 PM flight to Malta. Passengers on the Rome flight destined for Malta were told by the airline, before we landed, to meet a van on the tarmac to be re-routed to Malta. Upon arriving at the office I was told there were no more flights to Malta that evening. I was already 7 hours delayed from my original itinerary and was now facing another 10 hour delay and an overnight stay in Rome. While I was waiting in line to be given a flight departing the next day, I discovered another flight destined for Malta departing Rome at 11 PM. I dashed to the gate and purchased another ticket on this Ryan Air midnight flight. Hence I purchased two tickets for airfare to Malta (Frankfurt / Malta and Rome/ Malta). I arrived in Malta at 12:40 am so I spent a total of 22 hours reaching my destination. I was certain that Lufthansa would reimburse me for the very expensive flight I purchased from Frankfurt to Malta. Instead they responded that I could not be reimbursed because I used the ticket from Rome to Malta, which of course I could not use since our flight to Rome arrived 3 minutes before the Malta flight departed Rome from many gates away. Then I was told that I did not qualify for EU 261 compensation even though I arrived at my final destination almost 10 hours later than my scheduled itinerary and was eating my dinner at 1:20 am a day after my scheduled arrival. Then after seven months of letters and emails, Lufthansa finally agreed a 10 hour delay qualified for EU 261 compensation so they sent me my compensation but they still argued I had used their ticket from Rome to Malta so I could not be reimbursed for my unused business class ticket.. Since then, I produced evidence showing I purchased my own new ticket from Rome to Malta at 10:23 pm on American Express in Rome, well after the Rome to Malta flight departed so I could not have been on the flight to Malta that departed Rome at 9:00 pm. In light of this "revelation", eight months after my trip, they are re-studying my case to see if I should be reimbursed for the unused Frankfurt to Malta ticket. A few days ago they informed me they forwarded my case to Swiss Air, because they sold me the ticket.

title: They exceeded expectation"
content: Positive: flight departed on time. Boarding was organized. Staff was very courteous. They exceeded expectation. They handed out activity kit for my toddler and I loved that. My kid absolutely loved Lu & Cosmo, the Lufthansa kids birds (cranes?) He was devastated to find out we weren't flying Lufthansa on our route back. Food was ok, not amazing, not terrible. Actually, I wrote this review after experiencing an American Airlines meal, so Lufthansa gets a grade bump. Wi-fi is not free. I had to pay to arrange for seats. IMO if young children are included in a booking, there should be an option to seat everyone together regardless of where - I don't need front row seats, I just need to make sure I'm the one handling my toddler. Their in flight entertainment screen is really janky. The touchscreen sometimes worked sometimes didn't. They weighed our carry on before boarding. Thankfully I already heard they can be strict about this, so we made sure our carry on was quite a few oz under at home. But people got charged and they had to get their carry on checked if it was over weight.

title: the flight was overbooked
content: I purchased a business class seat well in advance, and when I arrived at the airport to check in, I was told that I was downgraded because the flight was overbooked. I told them to get me on another airline in the same class to get me to my final destination on time and reimburse me for the hotel I had booked during my extended layover. The only options they gave me were to either fly in a lower class or fly the next day. I told her I wanted to speak with a Lufthansa manager, who came out, and said that under EC 261/2004/EC, I am eligible to get refunded 75% of my flight price. Since I could not fly the next day, I was forced to fly in a lower class than what I paid for. When I got to my final destination, there was no customer service phone number to handle refunds, so I had to submit it online. After multiple weeks, Luthansa deflected to take responsibility, and after multiple e-mail exchanges over months, they finally said they are closing my case because I flew in the same class that I purchased. I had to actively fight and provide proof of my boarding pass in a lower class and my receipt showing that I purchased a business class seat. It has now been over 4 months, and I still have not received any satisfactory resolution.

title: the penny pinching is really noticeable
content: At the outset, I must say the crew were great as always. However the penny pinching is really noticeable, served Ribera prior to take off, cheap disposable headsets that were so bad I couldn't hear well enough to watch anything, and the quality of the screen is from a previous era. Basic wifi messaging access is chargeable on LH, yet something I've not paid for on other airlines on recent trips.

title: no information  about the luggage
content: On 6th May, I took LH631 and the first thing I experienced was unavailability of the WiFi in air. And then the luggage went missing at Frankfurt airport. There was no information or news about the luggage from the airline. Passengers were stranded near the luggage belt for more than 1.5 hours wondering what happened. The beauty of the horrendous Frankfurt airport is that one has to step out of the luggage belt area to inquire about luggage. When all passengers finally decided to walk from gate A to Gate B to report this, the lady at the counter said oh the staff just found a container that they forgot after offloading from the plane . LH took zero accountability or responsibility to inform or apologize to passengers.

title: I was impressed overall"
content: My first time flying on Lufhansa and I must say I was impressed overall. Ground service was OK while on board was excellent. Crew were great while food was sufficient enough for the six-hour flight. The only drawback for me was the bus boarding on FRA-RUH. Other than that everything was above the mark.

title: metal box taking up my leg room
content: In September 2024 I had a metal box taking up my leg room on an 11 hour flight which was attached to the underneath of the seat. I raised it with the cabin crew and was told I couldn't move. I raised a complaint with Lufthansa afterwards, and received stock template responses from people who clearly didn't ready or understand the issue whatsoever. They ignored my requests for the complaint to be escalated to a manger and then closed the case and stopped responding.

title: a nice flight attendant
content: Long flight in their outdated business class. Seated in the upper deck, which always is a nice experience. Soft product just ok (not overwhelming) and luckily I had a nice flight attendant, which made the whole experience acceptable. Purser was rather more interested to engage with Brazilian guests, maybe to show others he spoke Portuguese.

title: uninspiring and not interested crew
content: Rarely have seen such an uninspiring and not interested crew. The the buy on board service didn t have any of the sandwiches on board. No apologies, no willingness to try to accommodate, no eye-contact (not to mention a smile). Even with the obliging much paying customers to buy on board anything more than a mini mini bottle of water and a ridiculous small chocolate, clearly also in this domain Lufthansa is here amateur! Now, besides the fact I really fly a lot (50+ flights/yr with LH-Group) with this airline, sometime I wonder why I am doing this to me.

title: made to board a plane via bus
content: During my arrival and transfer in Frankfurt. We were made to board a plane via bus on the tarmac and deboard due to cancellation. This was after a 4 hour delay. I was then re routed to Brussels making me a day late for Passover in Israel. Very poor customer service. Only chat bot available to handle issues.

title: I no longer can recommend this airline
content: I have significant international travel experience having been to over 40 countries. During the last 2+ years we fly only business class because of a medical service animal that is an Australian Shepard that has to be with my daughter. Since flying with her (the medical service animal) we have used British Airways, United, Air France (received a commendation certificate), KLM, Air Canada/West Jet, Porter and Ryanair. I always provide all the documents: medical service dog, Dr. requirement, Canadian Certification, Trainers letter, Export Document (CFIA certified), vaccine and blood work record (CFIA certified), health certificate and of course breed/weight and no incontinence for long periods of travel some up to 20 hours. I had not flown Lufthansa in roughly a decade and decided to give them a try since historically they had been great. What a shock! Their response was very slow taking over a week and then denied her requesting documents that were already provided to medical service. I resent the documents only to be left at pending. I called the Vancouver International Airport the day before our April 13th flight and was told she was confirmed. We arrive at the airport only to find out her status had not changed and being a Sunday no one was available in medical service to assist. We waited nearly 2 hours while Lufthansa workers tried desperately to help us without luck. Finally, a manager came down and said Lufthansa listed no documents received. At this point we are in panic mode but through her review of the documents asked me to email them directly to her. Upon receipt she removed the pending and we were able to board our flight to Croatia. This could have been catastrophic for both service animal and my daughter. I want to thank all those in Vancouver to assisted us and all on board attendance for their kindness and professionalism. I no longer can recommend this airline not only for their disregard for passenger health but also the quality of the pods, I have always only experience great sleep in business class and these were obviously very dated and once reclined I need attendant manual assistance to resume to the up right position several times. The over 9 hour flight had below average quality of food but non of this is reflective of the staff we actually met and spoke with but are all airline issues.

title: What a disappointing experience
content: What a disappointing experience. Our outbound flight was on SwissAir and the quality of the cabin and service was equal to Singapore Airlines which was code-shared. We were expecting something similar on our homebound flight with Lufthansa but as soon as we boarded it became obvious that this wasn't to be the case. The plane was old, I haven't seen one like it for at least ten years. We were in the upstairs cabin but there was no privacy between the seats and there was, bizarrely, a service station halfway down the cabin, so the stewardesses were plating food and scraping leftovers directly in front of our seats. The screens were small and not touch-screen so you had to use a wired hand-held device to move a cursor on the screen. I gave up trying to scroll through the options as it was so unresponsive. The food was uninspired. The flight staff were pleasant but not particularly friendly and we didn't see them at all between dinner and breakfast. They didn't help to set up our beds which were so narrow that you couldn't turn over and the bedding was thin and not very comfy. There was only one bathroom for 18 seats and the waste bin hadn't been emptied overnight so in the morning there was no space for towels etc. Suffice to say that we will not be flying Lufthansa business class again.

title: not given me a cent of reimbursement
content: I recently (March 2025) was on a business trip from US to Europe. The original flight was cancelled due to a strike in Frankfurt. I fly a lot, so not the first time. But this is when several red flags began to happen. The first red flag was Lufthansa did not offer an alternative flight. The only way to communicate with them was through a chat bot, there was no real person to talk to. The only options I had was to refund my flight and completely new book, which would have been insanely expensive or to update to another flight two days later for an additional $650. Why Lufthansa did not offer this update for free was beyond me to understand. Lufthansa refused to reimburse me these $650. I ended up paying more than $2,500 for a flight ticket from the East Coast of US to Frankfurt. But it gets better. The first flight was a leg from Raleigh to Newark. That flight was delayed, which then led to me being rebooked on a Lufthansa flight from Newark to Munich, which also was delayed, which then led again for me to be rebooked on a different flight from Munich to Frankfurt. The funny part is that a United customer service agent was able to rebook me last minute on a flight to Germany on Lufthansa. Something that Lufthansa was not able to do two days earlier when their flight was cancelled! This last-minute rebooking of course also led to a downgrade of my booked class. I flew Economy. Over this entire process, Lufthansa has lost my luggage. At that point, I only had two days in Europe anyways, but all the appointments that I cramped into those two days had to be cancelled, because I literally only had one change of cloth with me. Thankfully my mother lives in Germany, so I could rent a car and drive to my mother to stay until my luggage was found. At that point, I already has another flight to Asia, so I lost an entire week of work, thanks to Lufthansa. At the end of the day, I paid more than $2,500 for an economy class flight from Raleigh to Frankfurt, that was more than 48 hours too late, and during which my luggage was lost. Lufthansa has not given me a cent of reimbursement. Dealing with their customer service, which you only can reach via web portal, is an absolute pain. They expect you to know all the Lufthansa specific rules and regulations. It seems their main aim is not to satisfy their customer, but to save as much money as possible. I travel a lot for work, and I flew many many airlines in my life. I always looked forward to fly Lufthansa. But this experience has changed my mind. There are many other airlines that fly to Europe. They all have to deal with strikes, they all lose luggage sometimes. But every single airline I ever flew with, even the US based airlines, were friendlier and more helpful in their customer relations than Lufthansa. Lufthansa just lost a customer in me. And I don't wish for anyone to have the same experience.

title: still get the same amenities
content: Even though it was a short early morning flight, I still had a good experience with Lufthansa. I still get the same amenities, like bottled water and a Lindt Milk Chocolate bar with the Lufthansa logo. Other than that, there is nothing incredibly major to explain regarding issues.

title: what a finishing touch
content: It's been seven years since I last flew on Lufthansa. I was fortunate enough to fly on Lufthansa for the first time in seven years, and the moment I got on that plane, I felt good. Because it was a short flight, I didn't have to worry about anything. But the best part was that I got a Milk Chocolate bar from Lindt, and what's nice is that they have a Lufthansa logo on it - what a finishing touch.

title: I have received nothing
content: Not only did Lufthansa cancel our flight two days in a row, we had to deal with an incredible amount of chaos and confusion. At one point, TSA had to send us back to the Lufthansa counter because we were given boarding passes for the wrong day. It was the same chaos on every leg of our flights. In Naples, the gate attendant was rude and made no effort to have the plane board in an orderly fashion. Lufthansa promised to pay for at least a portion of the cost of the hotel room I booked when our first flight was cancelled. It has been almost three months since they promised to compensate me and I have received nothing. When I contacted them to ask about it, it took nearly a month for them to send me a form letter saying, "We're looking into it." I have heard nothing since and I have no hope of ever receiving the promised compensation.

title: they don't have alternative flights
content: On February 20th we travel from Zagreb to Dubai via Frankfurt, service, food, movie options etc was horrible. They cancelled our flights on March 1st and they just rebooked us on March 2nd without our permission. I decided to call their customer service by phone to find alternative flight on March 1st but they told me that they don't have alternative flights on March 1st. I tried to change my final destination to Venice or Ljubljana just to fly on same day as per our original schedule, but customer service said there was no any alternative options. So my family had been rebooked for the next day but without any accommodation. They told me that they will reimburse me for hotel. I have not request reimbursement yet but i will post new review if all will go smooth or i will need to spend days and days to solve my problems.

title: no-service experience"
content: A poor low cost experience with full service airline Lufthansa, for which I paid a high fare. Got a cup of water and a chocolate (10 gr). Kindly enough they let me have a second chocolate (because I nicely asked). In their Tourist Class (not that their intra-European Business Class is anything great much better), Lufthansa (& Co.) are nothing better than any other low cost airline when flying in Europe. The only difference is that most of the European low cost airlines are consistent, you get what you pay for and there are no surprises. Not with Lufthansa: pay higher fares, add luggage, seats etc and get the full no-service experience!

title: Wi-Fi never worked
content: Tokyo to Munich, more than 14h airborne. Legroom on A350 of Lufthansa is comparable to Ryanair. Pure and simple. However they thought a steep reclining angle of the seats would make it better. All you will get is your IFE screen 5cm from your head with no ability to grab anything from the bag under the seat in front. Lufthansa should be ashamed of themselves. Wi-Fi never worked, you couldn t even connect and trigger the captive login page. They did not try to fix it and didn t seem to care much as it is not free anyway . Well text messaging is free and that s what 90% of people want. Generally the crew was unpleasant, dismissive, and looked like they would rather be anywhere other than here. IFE was non responsive at first, had to be rebooted, and was still the slowest IFE I experienced but at least it works. However selection of content was decent with a lot of choice. 5 Had a look at the premium economy section when booking, and considered upgrading. However, when on the plane, it looks just as miserable as our section. There is not even a clear separation between the two, and you re better off trying to get an extra legroom seat in economy. On the return of the same trip, almost missed my correspondence for the same reason.

title: they would not cover the full amount
content: According to their service desk, Lufthansa did not transfer my bags to Frankfurt, and therefore, I was going to be without clothes and essentials for 3 days. I verified with them that I could buy clothing because I had business meetings and would be reimbursed. Upon returning home and submitting receipts for reasonable clothing, although it was not comparable to my own, they delayed. I received an email from their customer relations that they would not cover the full amount, which was only approximately $700 USD. Instead, they only authorized $460 USD. I will make every effort moving forward to avoid having to fly with Lufthansa as they apparently do not keep their word on reimbursement and you will be stuck paying out of pocket for their mistakes.

title: service was very poor
content: My experience during this Christmas Eve flight was horrible. Of course comfort of seats is very good. But service was very poor. Flight has started about 22:30. So it was very late for dinner. I think everybody agree that we pay for excellent food and service quality during long-haul flight in business class. This is even more important during Christmas eve. First of all, chaos was visible among the crew from the very beginning. We were given drinks and snacks. However, then the drinks trolley was parked in the middle of the cabin and nothing happened for the next hour. During this hour, one stewardess was constantly engaged in conversation with passengers in the first row. Then the left side of the cabin began to be served by another group of crew, who were very slowly taking orders. Our right side remained unattended for another hour. When the left side of the cabin was finishing its meal, people came to serve the right side of the cabin. However, it turned out that there was practically nothing to choose from. The only options left were some vegetarian dishes, mainly based on red beetroot. Let me remind you that it was Christmas Eve. The stewardess, of course, was only expressing her regret. After another long waiting time, it turned out that other dishes from the menu were found and we finally we got what we asked for. Dinner ended at 2:30 a.m. Unfortunately, irritation and hunger did not allow for a pleasant time during the flight. I do not recommend business class in Lufthansa. This is very poor quality.

title: a terrible experience
content: Business class flights from LAX to Delhi via Frankfurt. The flight was delayed in Los Angeles, and we missed our connection in Frankfurt. There were about 15 passengers who presented to the boarding gate at Frankfurt. The agents did not care to help, and we were left to figure things out. The return flight from Mumbai was delayed and we missed our connection to Frankfurt from Munich. The flight attendants in Mumbai to Munich flight were unprofessional. Food was served on tables without mats, no syrup for pancakes, etc. When I complained to the purser, we were rewarded without worthless, useless phone coupons. It was a terrible experience. I would not do this again or recommend Lufthansa to anyone.

title: much more business class
content: My expectations were very high and I was disappointed because the entire experience was much more business class rather than first class. The first-class terminal was fine but no comparison to the first-class lounges of Emirates or Qatar Airways. Being taken to the plane in a vehicle was a nice experience. The plane was an old 747-8 and it was a nice experience to be in the nose of the plane. Strange enough business class p[passengers had the option of preselecting meals whereas first class did not. The service was typical German type- efficient but not warm. The best part of the flight was the comfortable flat bed which made me sleep for many hours very comfortably. The TV screen was terrible and IFE not impressive at all. Wifi was free for first class but speed was average.

title: would not fly with them again
content: Flew with them about 15 years ago and vowed never again. I thought that they may have improved. Alas, no. By the time that cabin crew got to row 30 (with another 96 passengers left to go back to row 41), they had run out of choice of food and there was only vegetarian left. I advised that I did not want vegetarian and also advised that I tried to book my meal online between three days and four hours before departure (on four different occasions) and was informed that the service was unavailable. I had the beef in sauce. My food was cold and disgusting, it was so acidic it tasted like the beef had been pickled. The meal before landing a mush of spinach and cheesy sauce with a yogurt and a yogurt bar. The food was hot but mine was inedible. The worst aspect of Lufthansa's penny-pinching on long-haul flights is the removal of the amenity kit. I struggled getting my shoes on in the cramped space so ended up going to the bathroom in my socks. The bathroom floor was swimming in urine. I hold Star Alliance Gold status with another airline and didn't earn a single mile on my two-leg outward bound with Lufthansa. I feel that they are using their dominance to dilute the benefits of being in the Star Alliance (they have already done that with the extra baggage which has led other smaller airlines to follow suit). The only benefit was the use of the lounge which was actually very good. Definitely would not fly with them again and am dreading the return flight - which is longer because it is east to west.

title: An average experience
content: An average experience especially with the ground crew. Flights were booked well in advance and seats selected so that the two kids under 8 years old were seated with an adult. When checking in we were split up, LH answer to this was well as you are flying business class the middle seat is empty so your child can sit there. Nice work LH it s nice that you pay to have an empty seat (this is really the only thing that separates bis from economy). We ended up talking to a passenger that agreed to swap seats when they realised they were going to sit next to a 4 year old. Cabin crew were friendly and the food was good, also for once the flight departed on time and arrived a bit early. On the return leg LH due to weather could not unload the luggage over the next 5 days the suitcases arrived one by one two days to find them and instead of them getting sent out in one go they arrived spread out over 3 days.

title: service has deteriorated substantially
content: Lufthansa service has deteriorated substantially. Only a bottle of water is provided, one has to pay for coffee or tea. Later a small piece of chocolate is offered.

title: provide us decent toilet facility
content: I am flying with Lufthansa for a long time and incrementally seeing the decline of the airline. IN flight experience is pathetic time when I flew from Bengaluru to Frankfurt. The toilets were so unclean and it had not flushed properly. I complained to the airhostess and said I can't use the toilets as they are unclean with bits sticking out. The airhostess made a comment 'that's how they are. They are like that'. I am not going to go in there and clean. What does this mean? Is it not your responsibility to provide us decent toilet facility?

title: crew was quite rude to me
content: The crew was quite rude to me when requested cup of tea and wanted me to take another drink, because was not convenient for them to prepare a pot of tea for 1 cup. Never heard something so ridiculous to says to a customer. My food was very bad. The entertainment was old. Also the connection from Munich to London we had to pay for food and drinks.

title: no information prior to approach
content: Graz to Nice via Frankfurt in March 2024. Polite cabin crew but some of them unfriendly. The captain didn t welcome passengers on board on route Frankfort to Nice as well and no information prior to approach. The tickets of Lufthansa are usually expensive with only one small mineral water on board and small chocolate. At Graz airport I asked to change the seat but their staff said there is not any available seat but another person before boarding changed the seat.

title: broken In-flight entertainment screen
content: I must preface this review by saying that I am grateful for flying on a lie flat seat on any long-haul flight for the cheap price of 1,100 but there is a standard to uphold for a so-called 4-star airline which I do not believe was represented by Lufthansa. My first leg was operated to Frankfurt, which was only a short flight where I was happy to see I had a blocked seat next to me as well as an okay meal. Our transit through Frankfurt was okay, the lounge was perfectly fine. My seat was on the window, but I was upset to hear that my seat had a broken In-flight entertainment screen, but I assured them it would be okay since I was not planning to watch any movies and I was sitting next to my family so I could just switch if necessary. But they insisted that they could not offer me the seat in a rather rude manner. I didn't question it, assuming that there was something wrong with the seat and it could not be given to me past just the seat. But after that I found out that it had a crew member not working on it, which shocked me since the seat was clearly reclinable and functioning. I do not understand why I could not have the window seat to this day, but there may be other reasons behind this I may not understand. The seat on this flight, while lie-flat was very thin and the leg-rest was bent inward, making it quite uncomfortable. The food was okay, but once again not up to business class standards in my opinion. Overall, I hate to hate on Lufthansa since I value them and hold status with them. Their sister airline SWISS is vastly superior in my opinion. I hope the introduction of Allegris will improve Lufthansa's on-board product and I can enjoy them in the future.

title: We had great flights and crew
content: We flew London to Singapore via Munich (and then onto Krabi with Scoot) This was our first time flying Lufthansa business and I had heard varying reviews. None very good! We had great flights and crew on all sectors- especially on the Munich-Singapore route and couldn t fault any of the crew. Meals were fine, not too restaurant quality, but very nice anyway and drinks were offered frequently. The lie flat bed was great and both my husband and I slept for most of the long haul flights. I understand that the 2-2-2 configuration isn t ideal and I wouldn t have wanted to be in such close quarters with anyone else - but as it was my husband it was fine. We don t fly business on leisure trips on a regular basis and only have experience of two other airlines business product, but I would choose Lufthansa again if their prices were competitive.

title: not enough overhead bins
content: Expensive fare and not enough overhead bins to honor carry-on baggage policy. Was screamed at to place carry on under my seat in front of me, leaving no room at all for my legs. Seat comfort on par with Ryanair, no inflight service apart from water bottle.

title: my food was below average
content: The aircraft from Vancouver to Munich was upgraded to the A350 featuring the Allegris business class seats. Although I was eager to experience this new product, it turned out to be a huge letdown. The Allegris business class seats are the firmest and least comfortable I've ever encountered. Everything about Allegris seems fancy, but they fail at the most basic aspect: comfort for sitting or sleeping. Lufthansa's onboard experience was lacking, my food was below average and no mattress padding or sleepwear provided. The experience at Munich airport for the Delhi flight was also frustrating. There was no queue management by Lufthansa, and the situation was chaotic. Families with children were standing in the business class line and taking over the priority queues. Passengers were arguing and fighting over queue cutting. The second leg of the journey, Munich to Delhi, was on an A380, and it provided a much better experience. Although the aircraft is 10 years old, the seats were far more comfortable than the A350 Allegris. However, Lufthansa s soft product remained subpar for the second leg of the journey as well with below-average food and no mattress padding or sleepwear. Overall, Lufthansa falls short of being a premium airline and does the bare minimum to offer a premium experience.

title: feels like they are laughing at me
content: I have been waiting for compensation for 16 working days and still haven t got it. I should have received at least after 10 regular days. I keep contacting Lufthansa and they always reply that they've already transferred the money to my account and I have to wait a few business days, but it has been a lot. They won't provide proof that the payment has been sorted. It feels like they are laughing at me and just waiting for me to get tired of complaining. I don't recommend this airline at all.

title: could not give me any updates
content: On the Flight to Europe, the business seat on the 27 Year old A340 was broken and could not be fixed and no lie flat. The food was average. On the flight back the LH feeder flight from Hamburg got delayed by more than 1 hour. All what Lufthansa staff in Hamburg told me was : you have to hurry in Frankfurt, maybe you can catch the flight. On the flight FRA-HKG i could not get a peppermint tea. I told the Flight attendant that on the flight HKG-FRA i could get a peppermint tea "oh well, then i have to check". Back in HKG, my baggage was not on board and came one day late. After complaint, Lufthansa agreed after a month to reimburse 50% of one leg ticket price for the inconvenience. After i sent them my account info, nothing happened. A chaser mail was ignored. The chat agent on the chat on the reimbursement microsite told me they could not give me any updates. Same with the telephone hotline. Lufthansa staff just tell you it is not in their remit and you need to call someone else.

title: 2-2-2 business class configuration
content: Old tatty cabin with a 2-2-2 business class configuration, great when travelling together but a pain when travelling alone. On the good note: lovely crew, great food and extensive beverage list. Inflight entertainment, so-so.

title: Airport experience very poor
content: Both transatlantic flights in out on 330 and & 747-8 respectively to Raleigh and Newark great service good, food good, staff friendly, seats comfortable, charge for Wifi messaging disappointing after paying for premium cabin .. doesn't happen on other transatlantic flights. IFE could be better. However Airport experience very poor, Newark weighing carry on bags at gate, ground staff very curt felt like a budget airline approach at Newark. NO designated bag drop only area at any airport, EDI drop off was a joke, understaffed no designated bag only drop off. Boarding process at Newark was awful. Frankfurt transfer awful .. ground staff very impolite to a lot of passengers .. then shipped out on crowded bus to A320. The connecting legs from Edinburgh -Frankfurt-Edinburgh were poor seats uncomfortable both ways. Overall if you were just flying the transatlantic legs Lufthansa very good. Flights to and from Edinburgh -Frankfurt not so good... would make me think again about going this route.

title: a decent experience
content: I flew Lufthansa from Bangkok to Manchester via Munich. Overall it was a decent experience. I didn t pay for the extra luggage therefore I just hand luggage. The seat was rather comfortable. The airline crew were ok, sufficient without be exceptional. The food was ok. The only negative to my flight was the layover was a few hours early on in Munich and nothing was open. I didn t have a great flight with Lufthansa but on the other hand I didn t have a bad flight. I would fly them again if the flight times and prices were right.

title: ended up wasting 2 days worrying
content: Where do I begin? The fact that I paid for business class was to save time however ended up wasting 2 days worrying over my luggage and 6 hours at the airport once it did arrive. You guys need to have the basic ethics of customer service & support during such a time where the luggage was left in Munich by your own actions. Further, I received a call from your airport spokesperson yesterday who then went on to make me wait 2 HOURS in front of gate no 6 at arrivals IGI Airport.
